name: Bastian Kuschel
text_appearance:
Male adult of 38 years, most, if not all previously identifying features destroyed or unrecognizable. Otherwise notably has greyed hair (kept short) and partial facial stiffness.

Typically wears a flex suit, loose clothing, and a piloting suit as well as gloves when on duty, as well as an array of modified gas masks (based on older Cradle models), braces, and wraps to assist in long term taskwork. Scars show evidence of heavy physical injury including but not limited to bullet wounds, previous undocumented surgeries, burns, gashes, and connection points where cloned limbs have been reattached. Missing a ring and pinky finger on his left hand, replaced with prosthetics.

Several figments of experimental neural technology are embedded in Nevada's body, including "node" implants at the base of the skull, down the spine, and in the palms and pads of his fingers.
history:
Bastain Kuschel is a name that has no traceable origin. There is no home planet, no sector of the universe, no home, no family which is missing him in their picture. The furthest documentations to put a history to a face only trace back to less than a decade ago, when one Bastian Kuschel was released from a hospital following a near-fatal piloting incident.

Previously under the employment of one Transcendental Office of Integration for redacted reasons ["Some drinks, a rifle, and something about roulette" -Unofficial Records, word of mouth], it is an open secret what happened in the same time period as such an incident. At the height of TOI's "New Dawn" Program ["It is simple, we want to tighten the bond between a pilot and an NHP. Integration, compensation for each parties weaknesses, creating something new. Something dangerously capable." -RE] several planetside TOI locations in the Ithia System were attacked by unexpected forces, including the Face of Death. The result of which was not only the end of the New Dawn Program, but a clearly devastating loss for the TOI. All accounted pilots in the program are recorded dead or otherwise gravely wounded, what little documents to be exposed giving little more. The TOI has considered future research into the long term effects of NHP cascades and unshackling while housed by the human mind, but thus far their candidate pool is too small to delve too deeply.

Whatever he was before such events transpired does not matter. He states little to no memory of previous life events and has no interest in them. Whether this is due to TOI's "reprogramming" or personal disinterest is ambiguous.

reserves.name: Bombardment
reserves.label: Resource
reserves.description: The ability to call in artillery or orbital bombardment once during mech combat (full action, RANGE 30 within line of sight, BLAST 2, 3d6 explosive damage).

actions.name: Bombardment
actions.detail:
Call in artillery or orbital bombardment:
RANGE 30 within line of sight, BLAST 2, 3d6 explosive damage

reserves.name: Sniper Team
reserves.label: Resource
reserves.description: LSA ground forces and Union marines are getting a crash-course in joint operations on this mission, and nothing builds teamwork like target shooting. You can spend this reserve at the start of any combat to call upon a nearby sniper team to provide you with fire support. For the rest of the scene, each time you take the Lock On Quick Tech action, your target takes 2 AP kinetic damage.

MechWeapons.name: Anti-Materiel Rifle
MechWeapons.description:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive” style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

gear.note: Almost always worn unless necessary/maintenance is required.
Typically gas-administered canisters rather than conventional injection-style stims.

mechs.name: Revelations
mechs.notes:
Personalization is a stripped down COMP/CON unit which will provide pointers and responses while active, but cannot act independently or control the mech to any capacity.

ONE custom paint job that works one round before becoming ineffective.

systems.flavorDescription: Mech-mounted Lotus Projectors are designed to launch small, actively camouflaged scout drones. The projector fires the single-use drones at subsonic speeds in bursts of ten, blanketing a wide area in order to relay information about terrain and targets within.

“Out, damned spot! Out, I say! – One, two. Why, then, ‘tis time to do’t. Hell is murky! – Fie, my lord, fie! A soldier, and afeard? What need we fear who knows it, when none can call our power to account?”
  • Shakespeare, Macbeth, act 5, sc. 1.

data.name: Ammo Case II
data.effect: 1/turn, when you attack with a Main ranged weapon, you may expend charges to apply one of the following effects to your attack at the listed cost.

ammo.name: Thumper
ammo.detail: The attack gains Knockback 1 and deals Explosive damage.

ammo.name: Shock
ammo.detail: The attack deals Energy damage. Choose one character targeted by your attack; adjacent characters take 1 Energy AP, whether the result is a hit or miss.

ammo.name: Mag
ammo.detail: The attack gains Arcing and deals Kinetic damage.

ammo.name: Hellfire
ammo.detail: The attack deals Energy damage and deals any bonus damage as Burn.

ammo.name: Jager
ammo.detail: The attack gains Knockback 2, deals Explosive damage, and one character hit by the attack – your choice – must succeed on a Hull save or be knocked Prone.

ammo.name: Sabot
ammo.detail: The attack gains AP and deals Kinetic damage.

data.description: Gained from the Walking Armory: Rank II pilot talent.

weapon.flavorDescription:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-I (T-1) line is defined by powerful, reliable, and conventional-kinetic ranged and melee weapons, including the GMS assault rifle, heavy machine gun, shotgun, pistol, and various light and heavy blades. Reliable galactic standards, the GMS T-1 line is the most widely used mech-scale line of weaponry across the galaxy. Echoing the Everest’s design notes, T-1 weapons are simply designed, with few (if any) moving parts, intended to be used in or adaptable to any environment.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

systems.flavorDescription: The hallmark of a well thought out mech frame is the opportunity for pilots to adapt their stock models to the specifications of the environments in which they operate. A retractable profile enables on-the-fly removal of extraneous protrusions, tuning of broadcast software, and masking of heat signatures – all serving to reduce optical and scanner signatures.

“Out, damned spot! Out, I say! – One, two. Why, then, ‘tis time to do’t. Hell is murky! – Fie, my lord, fie! A soldier, and afeard? What need we fear who knows it, when none can call our power to account?”
  • Shakespeare, Macbeth, act 5, sc. 1.

Mech-mounted Lotus Projectors are designed to launch small, actively camouflaged scout drones. The projector fires the single-use drones at subsonic speeds in bursts of ten, blanketing a wide area in order to relay information about terrain and targets within.
The GMS Type-3 Projected Shield traps and denies incoming projectiles by projecting an aggressive, superpositional anti-ballistic barrier.

weapon.flavorDescription: The GMS MC-AR assault rifle currently in use throughout Union’s forces and beyond is a time-tested and reliable design. Rather than attempting to replace it outright, CentComm's Defense Logistics Committee has put out a call for a supplementary standardized battle rifle system that can operate alongside the MC-AR in mixed fireteams, or as a primary weapon system for engaging heavily armored opposition at longer ranges than the MC-TR thermal rifle. The XC-BR uses an advanced caseless electrochemical propellant design capable of firing larger-caliber, armor-defeating rounds while minimizing recoil along with an integrated dynamic targeting system, though the weapon is still plagued by intermittent heat issues that can adversely affect performance.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.
